Women’s age and marriage: the trend throughout the 19th century Denmark

Ida-Bella Bach Greve & Kristine Xenia Storgaard Soendberg

18/05/2026

Introduction

This report analyzes census data from 1801, 1834, 1845, and 1860. The focus is on age, gender, and marital status distributions.

Kvinder (Women)

Women aged 20–30

1801

data_1801_young <- read_csv("census-1801-normalized.csv")

clean_data_1801_young <- data_1801_young %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(alder),
    alder > 19,
    alder < 31,
    !is.na(civilstand),
    civilstand != "",
    !is.na(koen),
    koen != "mand"
  )

# Histogram
ggplot(clean_data_1801_young, aes(x = alder, fill = civilstand)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "1801: Age and Marital Status",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

clean_data_1801_young %>%
  count(civilstand) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = n, fill = civilstand)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1), "%")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "1801: Marital Status Distribution",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  )

1834

data_1834_young <- read_csv("census-1834-normalized.csv")

clean_data_1834_young <- data_1834_young %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(alder),
    alder > 19,
    alder < 31,
    !is.na(civilstand),
    civilstand != "",
    !is.na(koen),
    koen != "mand"
  )

# Histogram
ggplot(clean_data_1834_young, aes(x = alder, fill = civilstand)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "1834: Age and Marital Status",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

clean_data_1834_young %>%
  count(civilstand) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = n, fill = civilstand)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1), "%")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "1834: Marital Status Distribution",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  )

1845

data_1845_young <- read_csv("census-1845-normalized.csv")

clean_data_1845_young <- data_1845_young %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(age),
    age > 19,
    age < 31,
    !is.na(marital_status),
    marital_status != "",
    !is.na(gender),
    gender != "mand"
  )

# Histogram
ggplot(clean_data_1845_young, aes(x = age, fill = marital_status)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "1845: Age and Marital Status",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

clean_data_1845_young %>%
  count(marital_status) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = n, fill = marital_status)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1), "%")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "1845: Marital Status Distribution",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  )

1860

data_1860_young <- read_csv("census-1860-normalized.csv")

clean_data_1860_young <- data_1860_young %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(alder),
    alder > 19,
    alder < 31,
    !is.na(civilstand),
    civilstand != "",
    !is.na(koen),
    koen != "mand"
  )

# Histogram
ggplot(clean_data_1860_young, aes(x = alder, fill = civilstand)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "1860: Age and Marital Status",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

clean_data_1860_young %>%
  count(civilstand) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = n, fill = civilstand)) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1), "%")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "1860: Marital Status Distribution",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  )

Men and Women Comparison

1801

clean_data_1801_extplus <- data_1801_young %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(alder),
    alder > 19,
    alder < 41,
    !is.na(civilstand),
    civilstand != "",
    !is.na(koen),
    koen != ""
  )

# Graph
ggplot(clean_data_1801_extplus,
       aes(x = alder, fill = civilstand)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "stack") +
  facet_wrap(~ koen) +
  labs(
    title = "1801: Age, Gender and Marital Status",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

1834

clean_data_1834_extplus <- data_1834_young %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(alder),
    alder > 19,
    alder < 41,
    !is.na(civilstand),
    civilstand != "",
    !is.na(koen),
    koen != ""
  )

# Graph
ggplot(clean_data_1834_extplus,
       aes(x = alder, fill = civilstand)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "stack") +
  facet_wrap(~ koen) +
  labs(
    title = "1834: Age, Gender and Marital Status",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

1845

clean_data_1845_extplus <- data_1845_young %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(age),
    age > 19,
    age < 41,
    !is.na(marital_status),
    marital_status != "",
    !is.na(gender),
    gender != ""
  )

# Graph
ggplot(clean_data_1845_extplus,
       aes(x = age, fill = marital_status)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "stack") +
  facet_wrap(~ gender) +
  labs(
    title = "1845: Age, Gender and Marital Status",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

1860

clean_data_1860_extplus <- data_1860_young %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(alder),
    alder > 19,
    alder < 41,
    !is.na(civilstand),
    civilstand != "",
    !is.na(koen),
    koen != ""
  )

# Graph
ggplot(clean_data_1860_extplus,
       aes(x = alder, fill = civilstand)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, position = "stack") +
  facet_wrap(~ koen) +
  labs(
    title = "1860: Age, Gender and Marital Status",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Marital Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
